---
title: "master_with_new_deaths"
author: "Zaka Hossain"
date: "2025-11-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

This is my attempt at:
1. Loading both datasets
2. Aligning column structures from new_recorded_deaths to match master index format
3. Joining the tables
4. Identifying any duplicates or issues
Not exactly in that order.

My memo: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1cDevx-di4XksnXoyi5OYhV8mA47_MiHQMpNRDPVCGOc/edit?usp=sharing

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)
```

```{r}


library(googlesheets4)

gs4_deauth()  
new_deaths <- read_sheet("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1LRSjhsrW1BJ2nKj78lGHR0C_8dxiaGhQFE2pzwSghx8/edit?usp=sharing", sheet = "new_recorded_deaths") |> 
     clean_names()

master_index <- read_sheet("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1LRSjhsrW1BJ2nKj78lGHR0C_8dxiaGhQFE2pzwSghx8/edit?usp=sharing", sheet="master_index_nov23") |> 
  clean_names()

deaths_no_dc <- read_sheet("https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1LRSjhsrW1BJ2nKj78lGHR0C_8dxiaGhQFE2pzwSghx8/edit?usp=sharing", sheet="Deaths without DC or newspaper") |> 
  clean_names()

```

```{r}
# Transforming new_deaths to align with master index structure -- keeps things organised b/f joining .. takes away a lot of headaches that would occur down the line

deaths_no_dc_aligned <- deaths_no_dc %>%
  mutate(
    # assuming these are all cheltenham related based on context
    facility = "cheltenham",
    
    # it made sense to assign personal_id after joining, so it will be done in a following codeblock
    personal_id = NA,
    
    # clean and standardize name
    name = name,
    
    # getting death year from 'When' column
    death_year = case_when(
      str_detect(date_of_death, "^\\d{4}$") ~ as.numeric(date_of_death),
      str_detect(date_of_death, "~\\d{4}$") ~ as.numeric(str_extract(date_of_death, "\\d{4}")),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    # setting death_date as empty for now (would need more detailed data)
    death_date = NA_character_,
    
    # birth_date and birth_year unknown for these entries
    birth_date = NA_character_,
    birth_year = NA_real_,
    
    # approx_age unknown
    approx_age = NA_real_,
    
    # adding source information
    errors = case_when(
      `Death certificate accessible on Find A Grave` == "No" ~ "No death certificate",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    
    # keeping it clean by combining relevant information into explanations
    explanations = paste(
      "Source:", What, "|",
      "Buried at Cheltenham:", `Buried at Cheltenham`, "|",
      if_else(!is.na(Notes) & Notes != "", paste("Notes:", Notes), "")
    ) %>% str_trim(),
    
    # comparing w record
    `comparison to record` = "New entry from recorded deaths list"
  ) %>%
  # matching w master index
  select(
    facility, 
    personal_id, 
    name, 
    birth_date, 
    death_date, 
    birth_year, 
    death_year, 
    approx_age, 
    errors, 
    explanations, 
    `comparison to record`
  )
```



**previous - new deaths**
```{r}
# Transforming new_deaths to align with master index structure -- keeps things organised b/f joining .. takes away a lot of headaches that would occur down the line

new_deaths_aligned <- new_deaths %>%
  mutate(
    # assuming these are all cheltenham related based on context
    facility = "cheltenham",
    
    # it made sense to assign personal_id after joining, so it will be done in a following codeblock
    personal_id = NA,
    
    # clean and standardize name
    name = Name,
    
    # getting death year from 'When' column
    death_year = case_when(
      str_detect(When, "^\\d{4}$") ~ as.numeric(When),
      str_detect(When, "~\\d{4}$") ~ as.numeric(str_extract(When, "\\d{4}")),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    # setting death_date as empty for now (would need more detailed data)
    death_date = NA_character_,
    
    # birth_date and birth_year unknown for these entries
    birth_date = NA_character_,
    birth_year = NA_real_,
    
    # approx_age unknown
    approx_age = NA_real_,
    
    # adding source information
    errors = case_when(
      `Death certificate accessible on Find A Grave` == "No" ~ "No death certificate",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    
    # keeping it clean by combining relevant information into explanations
    explanations = paste(
      "Source:", What, "|",
      "Buried at Cheltenham:", `Buried at Cheltenham`, "|",
      if_else(!is.na(Notes) & Notes != "", paste("Notes:", Notes), "")
    ) %>% str_trim(),
    
    # comparing w record
    `comparison to record` = "New entry from recorded deaths list"
  ) %>%
  # matching w master index
  select(
    facility, 
    personal_id, 
    name, 
    birth_date, 
    death_date, 
    birth_year, 
    death_year, 
    approx_age, 
    errors, 
    explanations, 
    `comparison to record`
  )
```


```{r assign-ids}
new_deaths_aligned <- new_deaths_aligned %>%
  mutate(personal_id = row_number() + 145)
```

# Checking for name matches (if any)

```{r}
potential_duplicates <- new_deaths_aligned %>%
  mutate(name_clean = str_to_lower(str_trim(name))) %>%
  inner_join(
    master_index %>% 
      mutate(name_clean = str_to_lower(str_trim(name))),
    by = "name_clean",
    suffix = c("_new", "_existing")
  ) %>%
  select(name_new, death_year_new, name_existing, death_year_existing, personal_id_existing)
```

```{r}
# Combine master index with new deaths
combined_data <- bind_rows(master_index, new_deaths_aligned)
```

#write to disk

```{r}

write_csv(combined_data, "master_index_nov23.csv")

```
